Photonic's Entanglement First™ Architecture scales up via dense qubit integration in a single integrated-silicon module, scales out through distributed computing across telecom-networked modules, and scales performance with fast quantum operations and efficient error correction across thousands of stable qubits.

Photonic's high-connectivity [Entanglement First™ architecture

T centres—a unique type of colour centre—underpin Photonic's unique architecture and were specifically chosen to deliver the distributed entanglement essential to high performance, commercial-scale quantum computing and quantum networking.

mentions.role_in_text: Developer of the Qubit Platform and T Centre technology
mentions.occurrence_sentence: T centres—a unique type of colour centre—underpin Photonic's unique architecture and were specifically chosen to deliver the distributed entanglement essential to high performance, commercial-scale quantum computing and quantum networking.
From the beginning, Photonic has taken a systems engineering approach—considering cost, efficiency, and ease of implementation as systems are scaled up.
Likewise, Photonic's system architecture prioritizes distributed entanglement using the T centre's built-in silicon photonic interconnect, enabling any-to-any connectivity between remote qubits.
Every T centre has a native ability to emit telecom-band photons entangled with its electron spin.
Drives Distributed Quantum Computing

mentions.role_in_text: Company developing distributed quantum computing technology
mentions.occurrence_sentence: Drives Distributed Quantum Computing
Photonic's Entanglement First™ architecture and advanced networking technologies enable any-to-any connections, delivering massive, distributed entanglement on demand.
Photonic's Entanglement First™ architecture and advanced networking technologies enable any-to-any connections, delivering massive, distributed entanglement on demand.
Stephanie Simmons, Founder and Chief Quantum Officer, Photonic

Photonic's high-connectivity Entanglement First™ architecture leverages a unique qubit modality, optically-linked silicon spin qubits, to enable powerful computation, efficient error correction, and seamless integration with existing data center and telecom environments.

Photonic's groundbreaking implementation of Quantum Low-Density Parity Check (QLDPC) codes provides efficient, fault-tolerant quantum computing, matching the best performance of surface codes and offering lower physical overheads, thereby accelerating the timeframe to commercially valuable quantum computing.

mentions.role_in_text: a leader in distributed quantum computing and communications technologies
mentions.occurrence_sentence: Photonic Inc. , a leader in distributed quantum computing and communications technologies, today announced the appointment of a new Executive Chair and four new directors to its Board of Directors, following the close of its recent $180 million CAD ($130 million USD) investment round.

Photonic Inc. is a leading quantum technology company developing commercial-scale quantum computers and quantum networks to address some of the world's most pressing challenges in materials science, drug discovery, climate change, and security.

mentions.occurrence_sentence: Photonic Inc., a global leader in distributed quantum computing, today announced the final close of over $200M USD ($275M CAD) in investment, giving the company a $2B USD ($2.7B CAD) post-money valuation.

mentions.role_in_text: global leader in distributed quantum computing
mentions.occurrence_sentence: Photonic Inc., a global leader in distributed quantum computing, today announced the final close of over $200M USD ($275M CAD) in investment, giving the company a $2B USD ($2.7B CAD) post-money valuation.

The breadth of investors demonstrates strong support across Canada, Europe, the United Kingdom, the United States, and the Middle East. Photonic is accelerating the path to fault-tolerant quantum computing through its Entanglement First™ Architecture, a unique approach combining silicon-based qubits and native photonic connectivity that enables seamless scaling across existing global telecom infrastructure.

TELUS (TSX: T, NYSE: TU) is a world-leading communications technology company operating in more than 45 countries and generating over $20 billion in annual revenue with more than 21 million customer connections through our advanced suite of broadband services for consumers, businesses and the public sector.

Since 2000, TELUS, our team members and retirees have contributed $1.85 billion in cash, in-kind contributions, time and programs, including 2.5 million days of service—earning TELUS the distinction of the world's most giving company.
